differential_diagnoses: Mesial temporal lobe epilepsy with psychiatric comorbidity
Psychogenic nonepileptic seizures (PNES) manifesting as functional neurological disorder
next_steps: Perform immediate lumbar puncture for CSF analysis, including autoimmune encephalitis panel, paraneoplastic antibodies, and viral studies
Order serum autoimmune encephalitis panel (LGI1, CASPR2, NMDAR, GAD65)
Initiate continuous video-EEG monitoring with specific focus on capturing behavioral episodes to correlate with electrographic activity
Obtain whole-body CT or PET/CT to evaluate for occult malignancy (paraneoplastic workup)
Consider empiric treatment with corticosteroids or intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) if suspicion for autoimmune encephalitis is high while awaiting antibody results
Maintain strict patient and staff safety protocols with continuous observation during episodes of delirium and agitation
